title: Dual sign-class induction: is the LA script distributionally logo-syllabic?
method: Per-sign distributional features (numeral-adjacency f1, solo-word f2, word-initial f3, word-final f4, mean-word-length f5) z-scored across the 61 signs with >=20 sign-tokens; 2-means into k=2 classes; separation = silhouette vs a feature-column-shuffled permutation null (500 perms; each feature column permuted across signs independently, preserving marginals, destroying sign-feature association). Classes characterized by mean f1/f2; 'logogram-like' = higher mean(f1)+mean(f2); between-class f1 gap + Mann-Whitney. Cross-site stability via per-site k=2 partitions on signs with >=10 tokens in >=2 sites, pairwise ARI (max over label swap) vs random-labeling null. Positive control SYNTHETIC (real-LB ideogram control unavailable): detect planted 2-class + single-class false-split rate.
result: LA signs partition into two distributionally-distinct functional classes far beyond a feature-column-shuffled null (silhouette 0.543 vs null 0.196, perm p=0). The smaller class (17 signs) is logogram-like: high numeral-adjacency (f1=0.475) and overwhelmingly solo-word (f2=0.808); the larger class (44 signs) is syllabogram-like: lower numeral-adjacency (f1=0.328), rarely solo (f2=0.119), composing into multi-sign words. The classes differ significantly in numeral-adjacency (f1 gap=0.147, MW p=0.044, logogram-like higher). The partition is recovered consistently across the well-powered sites (4 sites usable; cross-site ARI=0.095 vs null mean=-0.004, all pairwise ARIs positive, seed-stable). Haghia Triada and Khania independently recover the same logogram-like core (CYP, GRA/OLE-family, NI). Verdict: DUAL_SIGN_CLASS_CROSS_SITE_ROBUST.
key_findings: LA signs with >=20 tokens (n=61) partition into TWO distributionally-distinct classes far beyond a feature-column-shuffled null: silhouette 0.543 vs null mean 0.196, perm p=0 (500 perms), seed-stable.

The smaller class (17 signs) is logogram-like in pure distribution: high numeral-adjacency (f1 mean 0.475) and overwhelmingly solo-word (f2 mean 0.808); the larger class (44 signs) is syllabogram-like: lower f1 (0.328), rarely solo (f2 0.119), composing into multi-sign words.
The two classes differ significantly in numeral-adjacency f1 (gap 0.147, Mann-Whitney p=0.044, logogram-like higher), confirming one class is numeral-adjacent/counted and the other is not.

The partition is cross-site stable beyond a random-labeling null: 4 sites usable, cross-site ARI 0.095 vs null mean -0.004, all pairwise ARIs positive; Haghia Triada and Khania independently recover the same logogram-like core.

Positive control (SYNTHETIC, since real Linear B ideogram-recovery is unavailable) PASSED: the pipeline detects a planted 2-class split (perm p=0, recovered-vs-planted ARI=1.0) and has a single-class false-split rate of 0.05 (<=0.10).
successor_hypotheses: H1 (L2): Extend to k=3..4 classes (Gaussian mixture / BIC) to test whether the syllabogram-like class further splits (e.g. word-initial vs word-internal signs) or whether the logogram-like class has substructure (commodity vs person/animal ideograms).

H2 (L2): Test whether the logogram-like class is positionally rigid (always word-final/numeral-adjacent) using a positional-entropy measure, vs syllabogram-like signs which should be positionally flexible.

H3 (L2): Quantify per-sign numeral-adjacency as a continuous 'logogramminess' score and check whether it forms a bimodal distribution (two discrete classes) or a continuum; compare BIC of 1 vs 2 vs 3 GMM components.

H4 (L2): Cross-script structural control — apply the identical pipeline to a syllabary (e.g. cuneiform syllabic signs) and a logo-syllabic script's sign inventory to test whether the 2-class silhouette magnitude is diagnostic of logo-syllabic vs purely-syllabic writing systems.

H5 (L2): Test sign-class stability under corpus bootstrapping (resample inscriptions) to get confidence intervals on class membership for borderline signs (e.g. KU, KA, RO which fall in the logogram-like class here despite being common syllabogram candidates).

H6 (L2): Investigate the Zakros site, whose per-site partition differs from Haghia Triada — is this a genre/register difference (different text types at Zakros) or a genuine regional writing-system difference?
non_circular: Signs are treated as ANONYMOUS IDs throughout; their conventional sign-strings (including commodity-ideogram names like GRA/OLE) are NOT used as evidence — only pure distributional features (position-in-word, numeral-adjacency, solo-occurrence, word length) computed from the stream. 'logogram-like'/'syllabogram-like' are DISTRIBUTIONAL cluster labels (the high-f1+f2 vs low-f1+f2 clusters), NOT readings, meanings, or decipherment. No phonetic values assigned. The positive control is SYNTHETIC because Linear B DAMOS wordforms lack a numeral stream and marked ideograms, so a real-LB ideogram-recovery control is unavailable. layer=L2 (writing-system sign-inventory structure).
deviations: Positive control is SYNTHETIC (not real Linear B), as specified in the protocol: LB DAMOS wordforms are syllabic tuples with no numeral stream and no marked ideograms, so a real-LB ideogram-recovery control is unavailable. This is stated explicitly in all outputs.

Cross-site ARI (0.095) is beyond the random-labeling null (mean -0.004) and seed-stable with all pairwise ARIs positive, but its magnitude is modest; the cross-site signal is driven mainly by the two well-powered sites (Haghia Triada n=58 signs, Khania n=13), while Palaikastro (n=7) is underpowered. The frozen rule (ARI beyond null AND >=2 sites usable) is satisfied; the modest magnitude is noted honestly.

Null permutation uses n_init=3 for the column-shuffled null and n_init=10 for the observed clustering; using FEWER restarts in the null than the observed is conservative (it lowers null silhouettes, inflating p, biasing against finding structure).
